confirmed_fixes.note: analysis/meta_plan.json:181-190 now declares the plan packet schema, validator, skill handle, validation version, and narrowness criteria for MP4 output validation.
analysis/meta_plan.json:28-48 adds artifact contracts for input_objective_register and micro_deliverable_set_register, but MP1 and MP3 analysis outputs still lack field-level output contracts.

findings.title: mp2_drops_mp1_analysis_outputs
details: MP1 emits normalized_objective_register, objective_semantic_analysis, objective_dependency_gap_analysis, and canonical_objective_order in analysis/meta_plan.json:105-110.
MP2 then reduces objectives into DS# micro-deliverable sets, but its inputs are limited to canonical_objective_order and review_rationale_log in analysis/meta_plan.json:124-129.
That leaves the DS# register without a declared dependency on the semantic and dependency analyses that should justify blocking_dependencies, authority_boundary, and recomposition_constraints.
findings.impact: Micro-deliverable sets can be generated from ordering alone, which breaks the stated goal of preserving dependency closure and authority boundaries before downstream PLAN packet generation.

findings.title: bootstrap_replay_contract_is_not_machine_recoverable
details: The workflow marks objective_analysis_context (analysis/meta_plan.json) as an entry artifact in project.manifest.json:65-69, then requires objective_analysis_context for draft_meta_plan in project.manifest.json:97-108 even though the same stage also produces analysis/meta_plan.json.

Because the determinism rule says a stage may run only when all inbound dependencies are present in project.manifest.json:161-167, the packaged DAG models only revision of an already-existing meta plan and does not expose a first-pass bootstrap path.

The same bootstrap surface is further blurred because MP0 depends on integration_manifest, but analysis/meta_plan.json:59-61 points that source at repo://.project.manifest.json and marks it not_packaged_in_trimmed_archive even though project.manifest.json is bundled at the repo root.
findings.impact: A reviewer or controller cannot replay the initial planning transition from the shipped archive without inferring missing mode distinctions and source resolution rules.

findings.title: mp3_termination_metrics_are_unbound
details: MP3 now names a termination predicate, but the archive never defines how ambiguity_score is computed, what artifact family is hashed for stability, or how pass-to-pass equivalence is normalized before comparing hashes.
The gate therefore names conditions such as ambiguity_score_at_or_below_threshold and analysis_artifact_hash_stable_across_two_passes without a recoverable measurement or evidence contract.
findings.impact: Recursive analysis may stop or continue based on reviewer judgment instead of a mechanically replayable rule, which weakens the handoff into MP4 PLAN packet generation.

next_patch_set.title: wire_mp2_to_mp1_analysis_outputs
next_patch_set.why: The DS# reduction stage needs the semantic and dependency products of MP1 to justify bounded sets and preserve authority-boundary intent.

next_patch_set.title: separate_bootstrap_from_revision_flow
next_patch_set.why: The current artifact DAG cannot replay first-pass meta-plan generation without assuming an already-existing analysis/meta_plan.json.

next_patch_set.title: formalize_mp3_termination_evidence
next_patch_set.why: MP3 must bind ambiguity scoring and hash stability to explicit evidence so MP4 consumes a deterministic last-round analysis artifact.
